findings.title: Clean-slate / remove of a to-do place is silently reverted during the post-load 'cold' media window (deletes lose to the additive merge)
findings.rootCause: saveState() persists the whole STATE (incl. trip.markedPlaces) to localStorage, so on load loadState() shows the full to-do list before media is hydrated. But `_mediaLoadedTrips` is an in-memory Set that starts empty after every reload, so persistTripMedia() (media.ts:274) treats the trip as un-hydrated: it PARKS the write in `_pendingMedia` and calls fetchTripMedia. fetchTripMedia then does `_mergeMediaField(serverMedia.markedPlaces, pending.markedPlaces)` (media.ts:217) which is UNION-by-key — server items always survive, the parked snapshot only ADDS. A clean-slate parks `markedPlaces: []`, so the merge returns the full server array (every item resurrected) and flushes it back, also re-emitting STATE_CHANGED so the UI repaints the items the user just deleted. The guarding comment (media.ts:44 'During a cold window the only possible edit is an ADD — nothing is loaded yet to delete') is false: localStorage rehydration means items ARE visible and deletable before media loads.

findings.title: Two distinct marked places that share a name collide in the media merge — one is silently dropped (wrong dedup key)
findings.rootCause: _mergeMediaField dedups by `_mediaKey(item)` = `String(o.id ?? o.url ?? o.name ?? JSON.stringify(item))`. A MarkedPlace's canonical identity is `placeId` (the type's `id`/`url` fields are NEVER populated — neither toggleTodoListMembership (markedPlaces.ts:109) nor addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified (markedPlaces.ts:296) sets `id` or `url`). So `_mediaKey` falls through to `name`, and two different placeIds with the same name map to the same key — the second is treated as a duplicate and discarded. (Nameless items, e.g. an AI item with empty text/verifiedName, all collapse to key '' too.)

findings.title: Accept Plan orphans manually-pinned places' dayId — they silently disappear from every day's pane and per-day map markers
findings.rootCause: onAcceptPlan() deletes ALL existing numbered days (useAiPlan.ts:450-456) and creates brand-new ones with fresh ids `'day_'+Date.now()+'_'+idx` (useAiPlan.ts:466). dropAITaggedPlaces only removes source:'ai' entries, so a manual place survives — but it keeps its OLD dayId, which now matches no existing day. dayDetailModal._renderPlacesForSlot filters by `p.dayId === day.id` (dayDetailModal.ts:351) so it never matches; todoMarkers.ts:183-186 filters it out of every numbered-day view. There is no orphaned-dayId healing: state.ts only migrates documents/photos `dayId:null→anchorId` (state.ts:262-270), never validates markedPlaces.dayId against existing days.

findings.title: Legacy 🏖️ Sights icon is not healed for server-fetched media → a duplicate 'Sights' section + duplicate filter entry
findings.rootCause: The 🏖️→🏛️ heal only runs inside loadState() over localStorage data (state.ts:246). The authoritative markedPlaces for the active trip arrive LATER via fetchTripMedia(), which splices the server arrays straight into trip.markedPlaces (media.ts:204, 233) without re-running the heal. groupingIcon() normalises 📋→📍 but NOT 🏖️→🏛️ (todoCategories.ts:20), and 🏖️ is absent from CATEGORY_ORDER (todoCategories.ts:81), so a 🏖️ place forms its own trailing bucket. iconToLabel maps both 🏖️ and 🏛️ to 'Sights', producing two identically-labelled sections/filter rows.

findings.title: Day-Detail shortlist AM/PM/Eve buttons swap and de-localise their glyphs after the first interaction
findings.rootCause: Initial render uses the i18n strings shortlistBtnAm='☀️ AM', shortlistBtnPm='🌅 PM' (en.ts:1846) — sun=morning, matching _slotIcon at dayDetailModal.ts:304. But refreshShortlistButtons() rebuilds the label as a hardcoded literal `time==='morning' ? '🌅 AM' : time==='afternoon' ? '☀️ PM' : '🌙 Eve'` (dayDetailModal.ts:747) — sunset=morning (swapped) and not run through t(). refreshShortlistButtons runs on every input/toggle and on initial paint, overwriting the localised text.

findings.title: Day-Detail shortlist add/remove uses case-insensitive name substring matching — collides when one place name contains another
findings.rootCause: The shortlist toggle and its ✓ indicator detect presence by `textareaValue.toLowerCase().includes(place.name.toLowerCase())` (dayDetailModal.ts:744 for the ✓ marker, :852 for the add/remove decision) and remove the first line matching that substring (:862). A shorter name is a substring of the line written for a longer name, so the membership test and the line-removal both misfire across the two places.

findings.title: Hardcoded English strings across the to-do / marked-place surfaces (not localised)
findings.rootCause: These literals are written directly in JSX/HTML instead of through t(). Examples: Todo.tsx:643 'Plan with AI ✦', Todo.tsx:324 'View on Google Maps →', Todo.tsx:179/204 `Open ${p.name} on Google Maps`, Todo.tsx:241 'Hide details'/'Show details', todoMarkers.ts:157 'On your to-do list' pill + :145 'Day ${n}' chip + :151 'View on Google Maps →', TodoListPanel.tsx:373-374 Maps title/aria, dayDetailModal.ts:611 `${remaining} of ${items.length} left` (the initial render at :472 uses t('dayDetail.checklistRemaining') but the toggle handler overwrites it with the English literal).
